overview: Continue the existing alarm HGT codebase from the current implementation state and close the remaining gaps needed for a config-driven, miso-compatible end-to-end pipeline. The finished workflow must follow model_design.md as the architecture source of truth, use the implementation plan as the execution baseline, run export/train/validate/test successfully on a CPU-friendly smoke configuration, save checkpoints and result artifacts to fixed output directories, show normal loss behavior, and print <promise>COMPLETE</promise> only after all required verification passes.
goals: Finish the remaining runtime configuration, CLI entrypoint, and trainer integration needed to run export, train, validation, and test from a single command in the existing codebase.
Make the default smoke workflow runnable inside the miso conda environment on local CPU without requiring manual code edits or hidden defaults.
Persist checkpoints under outputs/checkpoints/ and result summaries under outputs/results/ for every successful smoke run.
Enforce completion only when tests pass, test metrics are produced, and either train loss or validation loss improves across a majority of epoch-to-epoch transitions.
Require the smoke run to produce test precision, recall, and F1, with test F1 at or above 0.60.
nonGoals: Do not build any UI, HTTP API, or web routes.
Do not redesign the heterogenous graph architecture away from the model_design.md specification unless a change is strictly required to match the specification.
Do not add real production datasets, online inference, or deployment infrastructure.
Do not optimize for large-scale GPU training beyond preserving a CPU-friendly local path in miso.
Do not expand the project into new research features outside the current synthetic-data-to-training pipeline.
successMetrics: conda run -n miso pytest -q exits with code 0.
conda run -n miso python -m alarm_hgt.train --config configs/alarm_hgt.yaml --run-mode smoke exits with code 0.
A successful smoke run writes at least one checkpoint artifact under outputs/checkpoints/.
A successful smoke run writes train and validation loss history plus test metrics under outputs/results/.
The saved test metrics include precision, recall, and f1, and saved test f1 is at least 0.60.
Either train loss or validation loss improves on more than 50 percent of epoch-to-epoch transitions in the saved loss history.
The final successful command prints <promise>COMPLETE</promise>, and failed runs do not print it.

stack.framework: Python package using PyTorch, pyHGT, and Transformers-style trainer utilities
stack.hosting: Local execution inside the miso conda environment
stack.database: Local filesystem artifacts: synthetic JSONL datasets, YAML runtime config, checkpoints, and JSON result summaries

uiNotes: No UI or HTTP routes are required; the user interface is the config file, the CLI entrypoint, stdout logs, and persisted artifacts.
The runtime entrypoint must log clear stage boundaries for export, train, validation, test, artifact persistence, and final completion.
Failure states must be explicit and machine-checkable: invalid config, unwritable output paths, NaN or inf loss, missing metrics, threshold failure, or incomplete artifacts.

importFormat.description: The training pipeline consumes line-delimited JSON sample graphs and a single YAML runtime config. Each JSONL line represents one full synthetic graph sample, and the YAML config controls synthetic export, dataset paths, batching, model hyperparameters, metrics, training args, and fixed output directories.

rules: model_design.md is the source of truth when plan details or implementation shortcuts conflict with the intended model behavior.
The project is an existing codebase and must be extended rather than rebuilt from scratch.
Only AlarmEntity nodes for ne_is_disconnected are trainable and contribute to loss and primary metrics through trainable_mask.
Synthetic samples must remain full graphs in exported JSONL; propagation logic must use logical masking rather than destructive pruning.

Feature width remains 32, the default HGT hidden size remains 64, default HGT layer count remains 4, default attention head count remains 4, node type count remains 3, and relation count remains 9 unless the model_design.md specification changes.
The runtime must support a CPU-friendly smoke workflow in the miso conda environment.
The canonical output directories are outputs/checkpoints/ and outputs/results/.
A successful run must save train and validation loss history plus test metrics before completion is declared.
Smoke acceptance requires either train loss or validation loss to improve on more than half of epoch-to-epoch transitions.
Smoke acceptance also requires saved test metrics to include precision, recall, and f1, with f1 >= 0.60.
The promise string <promise>COMPLETE</promise> may only be emitted after tests pass, train and test complete successfully, artifacts exist, and threshold checks pass.
qualityGates: conda run -n miso python -m alarm_hgt.train --config configs/alarm_hgt.yaml --run-mode smoke

stories.title: Lock runtime config contract
stories.description: As a developer, I want a typed runtime config contract so the existing pipeline can run from declarative settings instead of hidden code defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Create configs/alarm_hgt.yaml and alarm_hgt/runtime_config.py as the single source of truth for synthetic generation settings, dataset paths, batching, model hyperparameters, metric Ks, training args, and fixed output directories.
If YAML parsing support is not already available in miso, install it with conda run -n miso pip install pyyaml and wire the dependency into the project path used for execution.

Example: loading a config that sets n_hid=64, num_layers=4, per_device_train_batch_size=4, outputs.checkpoints_dir=outputs/checkpoints, and outputs.results_dir=outputs/results produces typed config objects with those exact values and no hidden overrides.
Negative case: loading a config missing outputs.results_dir, model.num_relations, or smoke split sizes fails fast with a readable validation error before export or training starts.
Add focused tests that cover valid config loading and invalid config rejection.

stories.title: Create config-driven CLI entrypoint
stories.description: As a developer, I want a single executable module entrypoint so I can run the pipeline in miso without manually stitching together export, train, and evaluation calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a CLI or module entrypoint that reuses the existing codebase internals and is callable as python -m alarm_hgt.train --config configs/alarm_hgt.yaml --run-mode smoke.
The entrypoint must create outputs/checkpoints/ and outputs/results/ when absent and must keep smoke-run artifact naming deterministic.
Example: running the smoke command on a valid CPU-friendly config logs export, train, validation, test, artifact save, and exits with code 0.
Negative case: an invalid config path, invalid run-mode value, or unwritable output directory causes a non-zero exit and does not print a false success message.
Add tests that prove the entrypoint can build runtime objects from the YAML file path without relying on hidden hardcoded paths.

stories.title: Integrate train and validation loop
stories.description: As a developer, I want the existing trainer utilities upgraded into a real config-driven training loop so the model can learn and report finite train and validation losses.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend alarm_hgt/trainer.py and related modules so one config-driven run can build datasets, use the custom bucket sampler and padding collator, and execute train plus validation with the configured model and metric adapter.
Persist train and validation loss histories to JSON files under outputs/results/ using stable, machine-readable keys.
Example: a smoke run over synthetic train and validation splits produces finite train_loss and val_loss values for every epoch and records them in saved history files.
Negative case: if a batch has no trainable positions or any epoch produces NaN or inf loss, the run fails explicitly and does not continue to a fake successful completion.
Add integration tests covering trainer construction, dataloader behavior, and history serialization.

stories.title: Persist checkpoints and test evaluation
stories.description: As a developer, I want the best model checkpoint and test metrics saved automatically so successful training can be verified and reused.
acceptanceCriteria: Save at least one checkpoint under outputs/checkpoints/ and persist a best-checkpoint reference or equivalent stable pointer for the smoke workflow.
Run the test split after training using the selected checkpoint and save outputs/results/test_metrics.json containing at least precision, recall, and f1.
Example: after a successful smoke run, outputs/checkpoints/ contains a checkpoint artifact and outputs/results/test_metrics.json contains finite precision, recall, and f1 values.
Negative case: if checkpoint save fails, the test split cannot be evaluated, or required metric keys are missing, the run exits non-zero and does not emit the completion promise.
Add tests that cover checkpoint path creation, result-file creation, and missing-artifact failure behavior.

stories.title: Enforce model-design compliance in runtime
stories.description: As a developer, I want the runtime path to obey model_design.md so the pipeline trains the intended target and not a silent variant of the model.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify through runtime tests that feature width remains 32, node type count remains 3, relation count remains 9, and exported samples remain full graphs with logical masking semantics.
Verify that only ne_is_disconnected AlarmEntity positions contribute to loss and primary metrics during the real training path.

Example: a mixed batch containing mains_failure, device_powered_off, link_down, and ne_is_disconnected AlarmEntity nodes computes loss only for the ne_is_disconnected positions while still running end-to-end successfully.
Negative case: if non-trainable alarms leak into loss or metrics, or if runtime dimensions drift from the design spec, automated tests fail and the smoke run is blocked.
Add regression coverage that exercises both mains_failure and link_down fault modes under the real config-driven training path.

stories.title: Stabilize smoke training thresholds
stories.description: As a user, I want the smoke training run to show normal optimization behavior and acceptable test quality so completion means something real.
acceptanceCriteria: Tune only the minimum necessary training-loop behavior or runtime parameters so the CPU-friendly smoke config achieves either train-loss improvement or validation-loss improvement on more than half of epoch-to-epoch transitions.
The same smoke run must save test metrics with f1 >= 0.60 in outputs/results/test_metrics.json.
Example: a 5-epoch smoke run records improving train or validation loss on at least 3 of 4 epoch transitions and saves test metrics with f1 at or above 0.60.
Negative case: if losses oscillate without majority improvement or saved test f1 is below 0.60, the run exits non-zero and must not print <promise>COMPLETE</promise>.
Keep the smoke config bounded for local CPU execution in miso rather than relying on GPU-only settings.

stories.title: Lock the final miso completion gate
stories.description: As a user, I want a deterministic final verification gate so the project only claims completion after tests, training, and test evaluation all truly pass in miso.
acceptanceCriteria: Both top-level quality gate commands must pass in sequence from the existing repository using the miso conda environment.
The final successful training command must print <promise>COMPLETE</promise> only after verifying result files, checkpoint artifacts, required metric keys, and the f1 plus loss-trend thresholds.
Example: when pytest passes and the smoke command succeeds, stdout ends with <promise>COMPLETE</promise> and outputs/results/test_metrics.json plus outputs/checkpoints/ both exist.
Negative case: if either quality gate command fails, required artifacts are missing, or saved metrics do not meet thresholds, the promise string is absent and the run returns non-zero.
Add a final automated verification step or scriptable check that enforces artifact existence and saved metric validation before completion is declared.
